full_prompt: Design a generatively designed desktop 3D-printable multi-function phone stand and cable tidy as an open-source consumer-FDM generative-design benchmark. The part is a single monolithic desktop object with a bounding box no larger than 150 mm x 100 mm x 120 mm, printable in PLA or PETG on a consumer FDM printer with a 220 x 220 x 250 mm build plate. The part must support a modern smartphone with a mass of 350 g (max dimensions 170 x 80 x 10 mm) in either portrait or landscape orientation at a tilt angle between 50 and 75 degrees, integrate a cable pass-through slot 8 mm wide for a USB-C charging cable, and include a small tray at the base for keys/earbuds. Material is PLA (yield 50 MPa, E 3.5 GPa, density 1240 kg/m^3). Pass/fail criteria: (R1) max von Mises stress in the PLA under the 350 g phone gravity load plus a 5 N accidental push-force at the top of the stand must not exceed 33 MPa (PLA yield 50 / SF 1.5), (R2) phone-cradle top deflection under the 350 g plus 5 N must not exceed 1.5 mm to keep the phone angle stable, (R3) total part mass must not exceed 120 g to meet a classroom-print filament-cost target, (R4) part must print without support (self-supporting at 45 degrees from build plate) and print time estimated by Cura-equivalent slicer at 0.2 mm layer height must be under 4 hours, and (R5) a linear buckling analysis under 5x accidental push (25 N) must yield a first-mode load factor of at least 2.0. Vibration, thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver an STL ready for any consumer FDM slicer plus STEP AP242 and a verification report with max von Mises, top deflection, part mass, print time estimate, and first buckling load factor.
short_prompt:
Generatively design a monolithic 3D-printable desktop phone stand and cable-tidy for consumer FDM (PLA), within a 150 x 100 x 120 mm bounding box on a 220 x 220 x 250 mm build plate. The stand holds a 350 g smartphone (up to 170 x 80 x 10 mm) in portrait or landscape at a 50-75 deg tilt, includes an 8 mm cable pass-through for USB-C, and a small base tray. Base rests on the desk; cradle supports the phone bottom and back.

Load cases.
- LC1 phone plus push: 350 g phone gravity + 5 N accidental push at the top of the stand.
- LC2 buckling: 5x accidental push (25 N).

Part mass target <=120 g; part must be self-supporting at 45 deg from the build plate and print in under 4 h at 0.2 mm layer height on a consumer slicer.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Generative design produces the organic topology inside the bounding box with print-orientation and self-support constraints, not FEA.

prompt.title: Generatively designed desktop phone stand
prompt.brief: Generatively design a monolithic PLA phone stand (<=150 x 100 x 120 mm) holding a 350 g smartphone at 50-75 deg tilt with 8 mm USB-C slot, passing stress, deflection, <=120 g mass, <=4 h print, and 5x buckling margin.

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: classroom cost target at 0.2 mm layer
